judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content (a map of North America/US coastline with a dense cloud of dots representing an impact footprint), but Description B adds specific details (numbered labels, specific caption text) that Description A omits without contradiction.
original_image_tags: A map of North America, rotated so East is roughly down. The map shows the outline of the United States, parts of Canada, Mexico, and the Caribbean. A large, dense cloud of black dots covers much of the central and eastern United States, extending into southern Canada and the Gulf of Mexico. The dot cloud represents simulated impact points.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Black-and-white technical map/diagram labeled “Figure 8. Atlas IIAS Impacts with Breakup,” showing a stippled circular debris-impact pattern over a simplified coastline map, with rotated annotation reading “Atlas IIAS impacts / Random-Attitude failures at 130 sec. / Thrust to 280 sec. / Breakup q-alpha = 5,000 deg-lb/ft².”*
